entry.title: Re: Create a Word Document with table in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: same issue here, i couldnt find a way to do that

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to get ALV data from standard program
entry.content:
You're correct, and the reason of the failure is that cl_salv_bs_runtime_info is using the RTTS creation strict mode, and included structure with suffix is not supported in strict mode. The only way (*) to make it work is to enhance/modify cl_salv_bs_runtime_info to make it run RTTS in strict mode.

(*) EDIT: as Raymond found out, there is a fix by SAP concerning RTPM_TRL_SHOW_FLOWS, they changed the DDIC structure TRLS_SUBPOSITION_I so CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO can now be used. No need to enhance/modify cl_salv_bs_runtime_info. 3288856 - CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO stops with runtime error UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION and exception CX_SY_STRUCT_ATTRIBUTES in class CL_ABAP_STRUCTDESCR - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA studio - SSO
entry.content:
Hi Lakshmanan,

You can utilize LDAP based authentication for SSO on HANA Studio to SAP HANA Database system.

The below blog has all the details how to configure LDAP based authentication on HANA DB systems. 

LDAP Based Authentication for SAP HANA 2.0 - SAP Community

Thank you,

Raj Kishor

entry.title: The meaning of associations dimensions in the AM model
entry.content:
Hello, 

The AM model I am trying to consume via OData contains multiple of the same dimension.
ex) CO_AREA →  0CO_AREA∞D19, 0CO_AREA∞D20, 0CO_AREA∞D21

What does "∞" mean?
How can I check where each dimension is coming from?

Hello ,

I am also facing same error , can anyone help?

entry.content: can you please suggest a code...

entry.title: What happens to links when SAP Hybris marketing is migrated to other system?
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are currently migrating Hybris Marketing on-premise system to other Marketing tool. I just want to clarify that what happens when a recipient unsubscribes from an old email is sent from hybris.

Does anyone have any suggestion for this that how do we manage permission from old email once we are fully migrated to other marketing tool?

Thanks,

Shivani

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP: How to call protected app across regions with SAML and OAuth [1]: Intro
entry.content:
Hi @CarlosRoggan 

Thanks for this blogpost.

Can this approach be used for multi-tenant application also ?

If frontend is multi-tenant (saas) app and backend is not. But both will have their own xsuaa and scopes.

Can I call backend API
1. From frontend application with user token ?
2. From frontend approuter with route defined for backend API ?

Your response will be very useful.
Thanks
Pradeep

entry.title: Re: The detail on alerts disapears after opening the documents on the detail
entry.content: Do you have to close the entire B1 client, or is it enough to just close and reopen the Messages/Alerts window?

entry.title: Education & Research in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
New Year, New Innovations!

Driving Innovation in Education & Research with the new Release 2502 of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

Dear SAP Education & Research Cloud ERP enthusiasts, 

As we stepped into 2025, the world of technology, and specifically AI, continues to evolve at an amazing pace, ushering in new opportunities and challenges for industries across the board. For Education & Research institutions, staying ahead means embracing cutting-edge innovations that drive operational efficiency, get the most out of research funds, and enhance the experience of academic and research staff, and finance departments. The 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, is designed to do just that.

This release represents a significant milestone in the journey toward an even more intelligent, cloud-based ERP tailored to the unique needs of Education & Research. With an expanded focus on AI capabilities, including the enhanced functionality of SAP Joule, our AI-powered co-pilot, institutions can leverage smarter, more intuitive tools to navigate a dynamic landscape with agility and confidence. SAP Joule now brings even more powerful insights, and delivers personalized, data-driven recommendations to staff working on research projects, grants, budgets, and finance, enabling them to focus on strategic priorities rather than operational complexities.

In this blog, we’ll explore the key highlights of the 2502 release, focusing on how these advancements empower Education & Research organizations to meet their strategic goals. Let’s dive into the details and discover how SAP continues to shape the future of public cloud ERP for this fantastic sector!

Cloud ERP core enhancements

Before we dive into the innovations within Grants & Budget Management please find below a range of new core Cloud ERP feature-based innovations for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the 2502 release.

Out of a wide availability of enhancements, I have selected three topics that I believe are very relevant for institutions of Education and Research: AI/UX, Sustainability, and Procurement. If you are interested in other areas of innovations for SAP’s Cloud ERP, please check out the Release Video from Bert Schulze:


Screenshot + URL: Get the top highlights of the 2502 release in this video by Bert Schulze

AI-Powered UX with SAP Joule

Joule, our Gen AI co-pilot, transforms user experience by simplifying tasks with instant insights and seamless navigation using natural language input.
A standout feature is its personalized card creation, allowing users to quickly access critical ERP data like purchase orders and delivery updates, empowering more informed decision-making, and insights in for example the essential goods required for running a smooth research project.


Screenshot/Video URL: Get instant insights based on natural language interaction with Joule.

 
Screenshot/Video URL: AI-assisted smart summarization


Screenshot/Video URL: AI-assisted easy filtering 

As we advance in AI-assisted UX capabilities, the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release brings exciting innovations in BETA testing, including:

  • AI-assisted easy enterprise search
  • AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home
  • and AI-assisted error explanation

You can sign up through this link to start your BETA testing!

Enhancing Carbon Accountability with SAP’s Green Ledger

SAP Green Ledger is a groundbreaking financial solution central to carbon accounting. It seamlessly integrates with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability platforms, extending traditional financial accounting to include carbon measurements. By applying double-entry accounting principles to carbon quantities, it enables businesses to track these on dimensions relevant to operational management, such as profit centers and segments. This approach provides a clear view of carbon impact from provisioning to delivery.

The core value of SAP Green Ledger lies in its ability to monitor, quantify, and report the carbon footprint across end-to-end operations, delivering auditable and reliable reports. By combining carbon data with financial metrics, it provides actionable insights essential for informed and sustainable decision-making.


Screenshot: SAP Green Ledger integrates seamlessly with connected systems such as ERP and sustainability.

Expanding the “Manage Certificate Receipts” App with Create Mode

The S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release enhances the "Manage Certificate Receipts" app with a new Create Mode. Previously, the app allowed users to manage and link quality certificates to purchasing documents and goods receipts, improving compliance and traceability. The new Create Mode adds flexibility by enabling users to record certificates received before goods arrive, such as those received via email, and link them to material records upon arrival.

 
Screenshot: Record certificate receipts before goods arrival, ensuring smoother inventory processes.

Grants, Budget, and Research Project Management

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2502 release delivers a robust suite of Grants and Budget Management features tailored for public sector entities, including state and local governments, cities, K-12 schools, and Education & Research institutions.

Building upon previous releases, the 2502 update introduces a range of new capabilities designed to support Education & Research customers. Below is an overview of the latest advancements, including related innovations for Enterprise Project & Portfolio Management.

For more details on this new global PSM scope bundle, visit the SAP Process Navigator to explore the complete list of available scope items. Direct link to the Roadmap Explorer: URL


Screenshot: Process Navigator focused on S/4HANA Public Cloud, and the PSM industry capabilities.

Additional countries 

With the 2502 release, the Public Sector Management (PSM) scope bundle is now available for US (GASB), Austria, Japan, India, Singapore, New Zealand, Belgium, and Switzerland, alongside the existing countries: the US, Germany, Canada, Australia, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

This industry-specific scope bundle, and functionalities, developed in collaboration with Education & Research institutions, offer tailored processes required for the financial management of sponsored research, grants, and funds/budgets. It also includes core functionalities for finance, procurement, logistics, asset accounting, treasury, leasing, sales, plant maintenance, contract accounts, and (grant-integrated) project management.

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: Roadmap Release overview

New employee financing using funds commitments

Overview

  • Leverage the personnel encumbrances in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud through the creation of earmarked documents
  • Enable financing of employees with their corresponding funding sources (funds, grants, or functional areas) which will allow the monitoring and control of budget consumption.
  • Budget availability checks when financing a new employee based on employee theoretical costs.

Benefits

  • Enable accurate budget monitoring and control of personnel expenses by allowing the financing of employees in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central.
  • Help ensure budget availability for payroll expenses throughout the financial year, including refinancing of employees when salary changes

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: Position budgeting Control in SuccessFactors with cost objects from SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

Additional flexibility in maintaining project milestones

 Overview:

  • Enables direct maintenance of project milestones within the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS).
  • Allows users to create milestones via the user interface or API for seamless integration.
  • Supports detailed milestone definitions for improved planning and execution.

Benefits:

  • Provides additional granularity in defining and monitoring project progress.
  • Facilitates milestone creation through APIs, enhancing system interoperability.
  • Ensures clear, well-documented milestones for audits and reporting.

 Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: maintaining milestones for research projects.

Additional items in the project cost report

Overview:

  • Displays commitments for purchase requisitions and inventory costs directly in the project cost report.
  • Provides both aggregated and non-aggregated cost views for better financial analysis.
  • Links procurement and inventory costs to project financials for a comprehensive overview.

Benefits:

  • Enhances visibility into project-related expenditures across procurement and inventory.
  • Captures costs at various logistic execution stages, ensuring precise project budgeting.
  • Provides clear documentation of project costs for audits and financial oversight.

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: research project cost report example

Residual-budget processing for Grants

Overview:

  • Enables either lapse or carry forward of unspent grant funds to the next fiscal year.
  • Ensures consistency by extending residual budget processing to grants, not just general budgets.
  • Maintains clear records of carried-forward amounts for transparency and reporting.

Benefits:

  • Prevents loss of unspent funds, optimizing resource allocation.
  • Provides better forecasting and control over multi-year grant spending.
  • Enable users to restrict future reporting of grant budgets on the current fiscal year vs reporting back into past fiscal years.

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: set up a residual budget transfer for Grants.

 
Screenshot: successful run of residual budget transfer process

Output services for Earmarked Funds

Overview:

  • Easily create and export earmarked fund documents in a standardized PDF format.
  • Ensures earmarked fund records are stored electronically for easy retrieval and audit.
  • Generates legally compliant documents with necessary approvals and timestamps.

Benefits:

  • Ensures earmarked fund documents meet regulatory and audit requirements.
  • Enables quick access, retrieval, and long-term digital archiving of financial records.
  • Prevents document tampering and ensures authenticity with standardized PDF outputs.

Roadmap link: URL


Screenshot: creation of the Earmarked Fund and the PDF output document.


Screenshot: PDF output from the Earmarked Fund Document

Staffing Resources in Projects Using Project and Resource Management  

Overview:

  • Enables project managers to create detailed staffing requests with skill, availability, and timeline specifications.
  • Uses intelligent algorithms to match employees to projects based on skill fit and availability.
  • Connects SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Project and Resource Management for streamlined staffing operations.

Benefits:

  • Ensures the right resources are allocated to projects efficiently through AI-assisted matching.
  • Provides clear documentation of resource assignments for compliance and audit purposes.
  • Reduces manual effort with seamless integration, visualization, and filtering for resource allocation.


Screenshot: matching resources to staff a research project.

Key Summary

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition latest 2502 release brings key enhancements, including AI-driven user experiences, embedded AI features, improved Grants and Budget Management capabilities, and the innovative SAP Green Ledger for integrated carbon accounting. And last but not least the ABAP Cloud Add-Ons also enables partners to extend and differentiate solutions effectively.

This release continues to drive efficiency, scalability, and sustainability in ERP operations for institutions of Education & Research. Please join us in the Cloud ERP revolution and explore how these innovations can unlock your institution's full potential. Stay tuned for deeper insights in upcoming blog posts, and engage with experts on our SAP Cloud ERP community page.

Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21st and January 24th, we conducted a series of engaging live sessions to showcase the exciting innovations in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.  

 Find All of Our Assets   

The 2502 release showcases 185 innovation highlights across the portfolio. 
Check out our release document for all the details!


Screenshot: Key Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release.

Thank you!

 Rob Jonkers - SAP Education & Research

entry.title: Call Hana cloud stored procedure from CAP java application
entry.content:
Hi Team, I was trying to call Hana stored procedure from CAPM Java project, which basically returns a table data after some processing done.

I have a Stored Procedure Test_Procedure.hdbprocedure under db/src/ of my capm java project name Test_procedure(param1, param2, param3).

Having a cds file under srv/src/Test_Procedure.cds

Having a handler TestHandler.java under srv/src/com.abc/handler/TestProcedureHandler.java

Can I get some idea, how to call stored procedure with input parameters using capm java project. I can see some sample for nodejs but not for capm java project. please someone help me to clarify how to do it in capm java?

Appreciate your help! 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: The detail on alerts disapears after opening the documents on the detail
entry.content:
Close and reopen the messages does'nt solve the problem.
I have to close SAP B1 or choose change company and login again

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
Hi Ivelina,

is it planned to provide application specific parameters via IPS also for the users ,

like Custome attribute1-5 ?

Actually this is only possible via additional RESt API.

Regards,

Uwe

entry.title: SAP Community Call for ALM Partners
entry.content:
Join the SAP Community Call for ALM Partners on March 12th and stay updated about what is important for you as a SAP Partner who is working with SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Solution Manager and Focused Build for SAP Solution Manager.

This call focuses on the recap of SAP ALM Summit EMEA and those topics that are mostly relevant for partners who provide services around ALM topics. 

For slides and recordings click here.

Link to register is coming soon!

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Schedule and monitor application related jobs
entry.content:
I was looking for information about this old transaction SAPJ. Good blog post, thank you.

That would be nice if you could add the information at the top of the post that SAPJ is now obsolete and cannot be used any more, instead Eclipse ADT is to be used. Pasting here a previous comment with the information source (which I confirm):

former_member212036‎
2022 May 11 12:00 PM
You cannot create your own catalog entries in transaction SAPJ any longer, this is reserved for reports delivered by SAP only. See note 2829891 for more information.

EDIT: sorry, SAPJ is not obsolete, the note 2829891 says "you can no longer use transaction SAPJ", which I interpreted incorrectly. I just realized that the Sections, Groups and Parameters are not displayed in Eclipse, and the only solution to see and maintain them is by using Open With > SAP GUI, the screen of SAPJ is then displayed and shows the Sections, Groups and Parameters. I posted that here: Solved: Missing Sections, Groups and Parameters in Applica... - SAP Community.

So, what should have said the note 2829891, is that the buttons Create and Delete have been disabled in the transaction SAPJ, but the button Change can still be used to maintain the Sections, Groups and Parameters. If you run SAPJ from within Eclipse, the button Change opens the catalog entry via the Eclipse editor, not SAPJ, so if you use Eclipse only, use Open With > SAP GUI to maintain a catalog entry.

entry.title: Bonus Assignment Statement-Adding Person ID and Preferred Name field
entry.content:
Hi 

We are developing a custom Bonus assignment statement and are able to pull the FIRST NAME and LAST NAME into the statement.  Instead we want to pull the Preferred Name(preferred-name) instead of FIRST NAME and also pull the Person ID field (person-id-external) instead of EMPID field. Unfortunately with no success so far.

Is it possible get these fields into the Bonus Assignment statement?

Should we get these fields into VP History file background element as custom fields and hide these there and try to pull them into Bonus Assignment statement?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Performant table list in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Hi @merituulimelkko , thanks for your hints! Like @Astradyne mentioned, we are very interested and would like to introduce this great table list in our first sap build app! We would be very happy if you could describe to us how we can create such a table list. Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Create SAP PM All Role
entry.content:
Hi,

We know that there is an SAP All to use all Tcodes available in all domains.

But, what if we want to have SAP All by domain? For example SAP PM All?

How can we do this? A solution is to create a new role and assign all Tcodes related to PM, but it is difficult in some way.

Can you share a good solution for this?

Thanks in advanced.

entry.title: Re: No Balance is available : Check the repayment while doing complete repayment in 0045 IT.
entry.content: Please how were you able to resolve this?

entry.title: Re: What happens to links when SAP Hybris marketing is mi...
entry.content:
Hello @ShivaniParihar 
This is a known topic for people who're sunsetting the yMkt application.
Recommendations: It's suggested that you keep the yMkt application ON for few months (preferably 2-3, anyway license cost has been reduced alot) after you've migrated all the Automation and Triggered based campaigns so that you keep receiving those interactions which you can later on export and import to NEW system. If you decide to shutdown the yMkt system immediately, you'll loose those interactions (email open/click/etc). 
If it helped, mark the thread as an accepted solution.
Thanks & Regards,
Kunal Bansal, SAP CX SME
https://www.linkedin.com/in/thekunalbansal

entry.title: Export data from app Display Credit Exposure
entry.content:
Is it possible to export data (cds view, odata service?) from the app Display Credit Exposure? We would like to have Credit exposure totals (open orders - 100 and open invoices - 200) in one table with Credit Exposure ( the business partner ID. credit segment etc.)


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Export data from app Display Credit Exposure
entry.content: The solution of this issue is to use the CDS View I_CreditExposureItem (CDS View Credit Exposure Items).

entry.title: Danish FIK outgoing payments via Idocs to IHC
entry.content:
Dear all,

we do POBO payments via Inhouse Cash. In future it should include local payments from Denmark (FIK). Those payments don't need receiving bank information (bank account) as the necessary information is stored in KIDNO field of invoice. The FIK nummer has following structure:

 

    The FIK OCR line is entered using the format +nn<zzzzzzzzzzzzzzz+yyyyyyyy<, where nn is the form code and zzzzzzzzzzzzzzz is either a 15- or a 16-digit payment reference. The last digit in the payment reference is subject to modulo10-check. yyyyyyyy is the creditor number. The following example is a valid format: +71<123450000012345+12345678<.
    The payext idoc cannot be posted on recipient side, error message IHC_IDOC639 (...determined transport type ... do not match). The message pops up as segment E1IDB02 BB is not filled with any data. I have customized the message as not populated in batch with new error message IHC613 Recipient account number/IBAN is missing.
    Does anybody has an idea how to handle those local payments in combination with IHC? The payext Idocs are created in a S4 system and send to an ECC6.0 system.
    The S4 system should be kept clean from own developments.
    Looking forward of your ideas
    Sabine Raschke

entry.title: CREDIT NOTE DUE DATE - SAP PUBLIC CLOUD EDITION
entry.content:
A credit note without reference to the invoice is always due on the base date. According to OSS note 17410, by entering a 'V' in the Reference Document Number field, the due date is correctly calculated. We need to automatically configure this field during the posting process between the SD invoice and the FI invoice.

In previous releases, the SDVFX002 (or similar) enhancement was used, but in SAP Public Cloud, we are unable to locate where to make this adjustment.

Any suggestions?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Need CDS View (released for development) for Resources assigned to a WBS in a Customer Project
entry.content:
Hi PS Experts,

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services, I'm aware of the CDS View I_ResourceRequestPlanItem, but this one is not released for Development. As a result, our developers are unable to fetch the list of resources assigned to a particular work package in a customer project. Using the app 'View Browser' I found out I_Projectdemand & related apps which have the ProjectDemandUUID field, but this only gives us the project-specific list of resources (activity types, personnel numbers) but not the WBS-specific list. Custom CDS views created by me (functional consultant) also cannot be used for development. As a last resort, I would have to expect the user to manually maintain the WBS ID in the 'Staffing Instruction' against each resource and then fetch it from I_ProjectDemandResourceRequest.

Kindly let me know if there is a better approach, thank you.

Regards,

Pratap.

Ref: Solved: How to check which Resource is assigned to a Work ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Catching connection error with SAPUI5 OData Model V4
entry.content: Hello,

entry.title: Re: GDPR Right to be forgotten (RTBF) & SuccessFactors Employee Central Data Purge
entry.content:
Hello @sandbisw , 

Thanks for this info, do you know if it is possible to set by company instead of for Country?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Catching connection error with SAPUI5 OData Model V4
entry.content: Hello, as described in the documentation : "The 'dataReceived' event is fired after the back-end data has been processed. It is only fired for GET requests"

entry.title: Re: The “onAfterDataEntryProcess” event in SAP Analytics Cloud Optimized Story
entry.content: Hi, It's very nice functionality indeed, though I believe there is a big flaw on the concept of storing the changes done in a variable in the story and then triggering the DA only for those changed cells. If the user leaves the story without triggering the DA, than you loose that context and once he/she enters back in the story, and change different selections of the data, the DA will trigger only for the most recent changes, so it will not cover the initial changed data. So unless you trigger always after a change, it is error prone and dependent on the users being vigilant and always triggering the DA before leaving the page. So I'd only use this function for data validation for now.

entry.title: Transparency and Trust - Project context
entry.content:
When a team comes together to kick off a project, it considers pre-requisites (inputs) and deliverables (outputs), both being tangible, technical artefacts. Let’s name a few examples:

  • Pre-requisites: code repository access, documentation availability,
  • Deliverables: new code branches and functionalities, changed software version.

A team with any previous project exposure will also reflect on past lessons learnt and apply necessary process changes.

Projects, by their definition, are short and finite sets of operations. A skilled project team member is likely to complete a few of these within a year. With a clear project plan, listing required activities across calendar weeks, it is easy to overlook an important investment, uplifting project execution. Let me tell you about transparency, why it matters, and what it will get you in return.

Cambridge dictionary defines transparency as the quality of being done in an open way without secrets. The project team could implement this quality while collecting project inputs, by adding information about when and why these are needed. This will not only justify the request, but bring more understanding, especially when communicating with an external party, possibly an SAP customer. Shared understanding will make the customer not only compliant but will spark the feeling of trust.

Throughout project lifecycle, project manager will compile and publish project health checks. These should build on the initial sparkle of trust and contain clear and honest view of the current matters. Team’s accomplishments and successes should be presented transparently alongside risks and issues. The latter, ideally, are assigned to an owner and given a timeframe to be addressed. I would advise that the ownership is shared among SAP and the external party, to provide increased support and again, foster trust. A trusting partner will be more likely to inform of their own challenges and difficulties and will appreciate and celebrate achievements.

On technical team level it is important that daily tasks are actively maintained in a shared workspace. Certainly, there would always be exceptions, especially in case of SAP-only accessed areas. However, these should remain exceptions. The rule of thumb should be a transparent backlog, upkept and collaboratively resolved. A shrinking set of pre-defined items is an evident navigation marker further cultivating trust. The path had been defined, and the path is followed.

Upon project closing activities, it is advised to hold a retrospective or a reflection session to collect team members’ feedback on the recent achievements. In case of external, or customer-facing engagements, these teams should be welcomed to join. In my experience, a transparently led project is leaving behind a very strong feeling of becoming one team, and a great amount of mutual respect and trust. In market broker language – the transparency is your investment, and the trust is your return.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Billing Document OData - Pricing Elements No Data
entry.content: The reason is that the access control of I_PricingElement cds is set to @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #PRIVILEGED_ONLY

entry.title: SAC - dynamic text needed for folder path
entry.content:
Hi

I would like to use a dynamic txt in SAC stories showing the folder just below 'Public' for the story. 

This indicates to the readers if the story is an official corporate story or not. We need an indicator that changes automatically when a copy is made but placed in another local location.

Does anyone have a trick for this which does not include using a template?

thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Billing Document OData - Pricing Elements...
entry.content: The reason is that the access control of I_PricingElement cds is set to @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #PRIVILEGED_ONLY In order to access the data, you can use 'with privileged access' keyword in your SQL command like I did below INNER JOIN I_PricingElement WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS. This is how I solved it right now.

entry.title: Possible to Apply Filter on All on Multiple Data Models?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

For a client, I am facing an issue in a story report where we operate with two data models. The first data model is named Own Workforce and the other datamodel is named Turnover. 

I am using the filter "applied to all pages". Two pages have Workforce as data model and the last page have turnover as data model. 

I noticed that the filters are only working on the workforce data model. 

When I try to change the filter to the other data model, turnover, nothing happens and the filter is reverted back to the first data model, own workforce. Is it not possible to filter on different data models when using applied to all pages? 

Appreciate all feedback!

entry.title: Billing block on billing plan
entry.content:
Hi experts

How do we restrict some users to remove the billing block from billing plan tab in ZPB orders?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: ODN number range from and to number length only 10 CHAR
entry.content: Did you get any work around on this..?

entry.title: Re: How to get ClientIP in Sap commerce cloud Backend.
entry.content:
HI, 

You can use the build-in logging system (OpenSearch) for that. The link is in the cloud portal.

In the OpenSearch website:

  • Go to "Dicover" and select the files "logs-json-*"
  • select a filter : field "log" is "access-log" 
  • to limit the endpoint, use the filter 
    field "kubernetes.labels.ccv2_cx_sap_com_platform-aspect" is "api" or e.g. "storefront" .

To see the possible values, you can just select the field kubernetes.labels.ccv2_cx_sap_com_platform-aspect on the left using the plus button to show up as column in the result list.

I hope that will help you .

Best regards,
Kai

entry.title: Re: Failed: NIECONN_REFUSED(-10) when calling external API from ABAP..working same from Postman
entry.content:
Hi Team,
I am also facing the same issue even we have uploaded the API certificate under STRUST.
If you already resolved the issue, please provide the solution or steps.

entry.title: Badges are not showing
entry.content: Why my earned badges are not showing in my profile that  i have earned in sap tutorial .

entry.title: Re: Consuming CDS Views with own namespace in SAP Analyti...

entry.title: Re: Bank to Bank Transfer (B2B) Configuration and End-User Steps
entry.content:
The above configuration you have explained described only about book transfer from one house bank GL to another house bank GL. This is only book transfer. Can you please elaborate how fund will move from one bank account to another bank account. How SAP will communicate with bank to transfer the fund from one Bank to another bank because as per your explanation fund has moved from GL to GL and not from the Bank to Bank. 

Please help to elaborate.

entry.title: Re: Reimagining digital learning as an integrated part of a Digital Adoption Platform with SAP &&&&
entry.content: Thank you Kristina for the collaborations and updates!

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content:
Nice blog, though one working version as suggested also contains its problems, and in my view, the whole concept of private versions in SAC is more confusing and posing more modelling challenges than necessary. 

 - In many companies, there is a rolling forecast process, though there might be quarterly and yearly reviews, where they need to work with different "working versions" at the same time (the quarterly along with the monthly, and the annually along with the monthly and/or quarterly). So the "simplistic" version management is not possible, since you cannot have parallel Budget and Forecast with only one working version. If we add two, than we might just add them all right? 

- Actuals into Actuals version - yes, agree, though in latest projects I've seen we need to replicate the Actuals into the planning versions as well in most cases, since SAC puts many restrictions on the "Version" dimension while creating the stories and data actions. For example, making FY comparisons with different versions for years where we have AC+FC in both versions is very challenging in the stories, specially if you add the currency factor to it.

- To allow real simulations using private versions, you need to assume all your artifacts, data actions, data loads, stories, logics are in one SAC model only. This might be the case for simple planning processes, though in most real cases, you need to split the modelling into different models due to very different data granularity requirements. Most big applications in SAC would suffer to have all the different aspects of data, rates, etc, in one single model. Maybe I'm totally wrong here and we should be having models with 30+ dimensions? 

Would you have different opinions on above?

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to get ALV data from standard program
entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi as someone being new to SAP/ABAP how would I make sure that cl_salv_bs_runtime_info is running in the correct mode if i decided to modiy/enhance it. Also is there maybe a different way to get the output data from another report (in my case RTPM_TRL_SHOW_FLOWS) without using cl_salv_bs_runtime_info that avoids this issue? Thank you so much for your help! You definitely cleared some things up for me! 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to get ClientIP in Sap commerce cloud Backend.
entry.content: Client IP address can be recorded in different headers (depending if the CDN was used or not and wich CDN). As you mentioned it could be "X-FORWARDED-FOR". For Akamai you could extract it from "True-Client-IP" header. Checking the ones from "X-FORWARDED-FOR" or "True-Client-IP" before actually using remote address from the request seems like a good approach.

entry.title: SAC customized usage statistics based on the dimensions defined individually
entry.content:
Hi All,

SAC is missing customized objects and usage statistics based on the dimensions defined individually by each SAP customer. There is no option to see the % of dashboards in Finance, Sales etc, as well as differentiate on what part of those is IT developed and what by the Business.

There is no additional story/model/insight attribute except of the catalog filters. Those would be working perfect here - as already enable defining own dimensions. Unfortunately they are only used for filtering in catalog and are not included in the system overview so we can't do any statistics.

Anyone figured out on how to resolve that? I have just opened the Influence request, so please vote as well if you'd find that solution fitting for you as well: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/340627/?section=sectionDetails

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
Why is SAP not communicating with its customers early around what we can expect in 1h for ILX?

Not knowing how all our learning homepages are going to work in a few months is very concerning. I am still not over SAP calling this new search "Enhanced" and then the legacy search works 100x better. We are having lots of problems with items taking multiple days or not appearing at all in the new search. We even had our entire ILX search filters change to SPANISH the other day and we only use UK and US english locales. And of course the ticket just went nowhere.

How can SAP leave all their customers with all these unanswered questions and all these problems that we will likely become the norm for us when they take legacy away in 1h???

entry.title: Accompanying Sheet for Payment Output in Contract Accounting
entry.content:
This blog post introduces how to create an accompanying sheet when generating a payment file in Contract Accounting.

An accompanying sheet is created containing a total amount of one or more fields in a file, or hash total. You can use the hash total in the sheet to compare it with the hash total that is generated when you upload the file to your bank portal.

This blog post takes the solution for Netherlands as an example to display how to generate accompanying sheets and import the files.

Business Procedure

Before we learn about how to set up the accompanying sheet functions, we have to know in which stage the accompanying sheets are generated. The following paragraph is the end-to-end business procedure.

  1. You have an open item, either for direct debit payment or credit transfer, in Contract Accounting.
  2. You execute a payment run for this open item.
  3. You select the payment run and download the payment file. You can get the accompanying sheet depends on your configuration in the output management, for example, through an email.

Setting up Accompany Sheet

To generate accompanying sheet, you have to complete the configuration activities as follows:

  1. Map Format Data for Payments: enabling accompanying sheet for the output setting in Map Format Data for Payments.
  2. Define Payment Medium Formats: selecting the checkbox of extra payment medium with documents.
  3. Assign Form Templates: Define FIN_FO_PAYM_HASH_NL_ACCSHT as the output form template.
  4. Assign Email templates.

Enabling Accompanying Sheet in Map Format Data for Payments

You can modify the standard mapping structure that is delivered by SAP in the activity Map Format Data for Payments.

  1. In your configuration environment, open Map Format Data for Payments (ID: 100558).
  2. Create your own mapping format or copy the existing one.
  3. Select Edit.
  4. In the Format Mapping Attributes, select either Simplified Accompanying Sheet or Accompanying Sheet with Subtotals as your accompany sheet type.
  5. Select Release, when you finish editing.

Define Payment Medium Formats

In this activity, select the checkbox “Accompanying Sheet for Payment Medium” that allows you to generate an accompanying sheet with this payment medium.

  1. In your configuration environment open Define Payment Medium Formats (ID: 106730).
  2. Create your payment medium format by copying the existing one. For more information check Enhancement of Payment Formats in Contract Accounting.
  3. When you set up your payment medium format, select the checkbox “Accompanying Sheet for Payment Medium” and selected the format tree that you defined in the previous configuration activity (Map Format Data for Payments).
  4. Save your entry.

You then complete the following configuration activities Define Variants for Payment Medium Program and Define Variant Determination for Payment Medium Program. To learn about more details, see Enhancement of Payment Formats in Contract Accounting.

Setting up Output Management

Assign an Output Form Template

In this activity you can define the output form or use the template delivered by SAP. In this activity, we take the Form Template ID for Netherland as an example.

  1. In your configuration environment open Assign Form Templates (ID: 102313).
  2. Search for the form template based on the following values:

    Field

    Value

    Appl.Object Type

    FFO_PAYM_MEDIUM

    Output Type 

    FFO_ACCOM_SHT

    Email Template ID

    FFO_PAYM_ACCOMPANY_SHEET_EMAIL

  3. Save your entry.

Assign Email templates

In this activity you can define an email template or use the template delivered by SAP for customizing outputs or specific application objects.

  1. In your configuration environment open Assign Email Templates (ID:101945).
  2. Create your entries or use the following sample values.

    Field

    Value

    Appl.Object Type

    FFO_PAYM_MEDIUM

    Output Type 

    FFO_ACCOM_SHT

    Email Template ID

    FFO_PAYM_ACCOMPANY_SHEET_EMAIL

Creating Accompanying Sheets for Payment Output

In this activity you can configure the output of payment accompanying sheets for your payment medium formats.

To access the Output Parameter Determination app, you need to have the authorization delivered with the business role template SAP_BR_CONF_EXPERT_BUS_NET_INT (Configuration Expert - Business Network Integration).

  1. Open the Output Parameter Determination app.
  2. Show Rules for Payment Medium.
  3. In the determination step select the following options successively and create entries as the sample values for Netherlands. Alternatively, you can create entries based on your business requirements.
  4. Activate the settings.

After you finished the settings, you can run the standard business procedure that is mentioned above and get the accompanying sheet through the email or you can use app  "Manage Output Items" to view your accompanying sheets output.

Is the blog post useful to you? Leave your comments to let us know your thoughts!

entry.title: Missing Sections, Groups and Parameters in Application Job Catalog Entry (Eclipse ADT/Private Cloud)
entry.content:
Hello, I'm wondering why I can't see the Sections, Groups and Parameters in my S/4HANA Private Cloud system (should be appearing at the bottom):

I'm trying with an S/4HANA Public Cloud system, I can see them:

Also, in the old transaction code SAPJ (old S/4HANA system), we could see them (copyright S/4 HANA Schedule and monitor application related ... - SAP Community😞

I'm asking because SAPJ cannot be used anymore (2829891 - Application Jobs in On Premise systems - SAP for Me), Eclipse is to be used instead, but I guess all features from SAPJ should be available in Eclipse too.

Eclipse / ADT version: 2024-12 / 3.46

Backend: S/4HANA ON PREMISE 2023 / ABAP 7.58 SP 0

Thank you.

Sandra

Stay up to date with new and enhanced functionality in SAP Cloud ALM. The updates look back at what has been delivered in the previous quarter.

Learn directly from subject matter experts about what's new and have your questions answered!

For slides and recordings click here. 

Link to register is coming soon!

entry.title: Re: Manager Form Eligibility Rules - Variable Pay Management
entry.content: Hi @xavierlegarrec, thank you very much for your feedback. This is good news for us. We added the Manager Form Eligibility rule based on Hire Date (if "Hire Date ≥01/01/2025 --> "Compensation: No"). Also we updated the Hire Date of a test user in the UDF file, so they would be excluded (Hire Date = 01.08.2025). Unfortunately the user is still visible on our worksheets after applying the rule. Do you have any recommendation what we could try or what could be the reason? Thank you very much in advance and best regards, Ann-Kathrin

entry.title: SAP MaxDB - DBM:Test Event not invoked (Event Dispatcher)
entry.content:
Dear All, we have enabled the SAP MaxDB Event Dispatcher and setup the SDB Mail Configuration. We have also created the Event DBM:Test with condition Priority = LOW but it seems to be never invoked.

Looking at the logs it seems there are no errors.

Has anyone faced this issue? Any suggestion?

SAP MaxDB

entry.title: Re: Configuring the Remote ATC Central Check System in a...
entry.content:
Hello Sharyu,

yes, this is possible.

There are 3 options for the ATC Hub placement:

  • S/4HANA development system*
  • S/4HANA test-system
  • Separate ATC hub system.

BR, 
Christoph

entry.title: Re: SAP SQL Anywhere RESTORE with RENAME - What is the co...
entry.content:
Hello, Chris!
Thanks for providing the reply

Two comments from my side:
Comment #1.

  • If you need to rename the backup system dbspace, that cannot be done with the RESTORE statement.  I would us xp_move_file or something at the OS level to perform that rename - I don't want to rename "dbspace" I want to map to newly created files with another name

Comment #2. I'm trying to understand what "a_restore" means -> because it looks like this error happens after restore is completed and database server is trying to complete it.
My next steps will be - Stop database server which is running and try to run my restore with changing file names

entry.title: Re: Missing Sections, Groups and Parameters in Applicatio...
entry.content:
The sections, groups and parameters can be accessed from ADT via Open With > SAP GUI:

It's then possible to maintain the sections, groups and parameters (NB: it's the transaction SAPJ, but after note 2829891 - Application Jobs in On Premise systems - SAP for Me is applied, this screen of the transaction can be accessed only via Eclipse/ADT):

I don't really understand the reason why the sections, groups and parameters cannot be seen in Eclipse ADT, because it works fine if I connect to an S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

entry.title: Using the Migration Project in Different Client
entry.content:
LTMOM Changes

 

 S4D 100 :T-CODE-> LTMOM

 

We created one project-> * and we are trying to map some of the fields from source to target structure in the LTMC Template.

 

After completion of adding all the required fields, we have saved and created TR for using the same project in another client (S4D 300) and we have moved it to that client. Now, we want to make some changes in the same project in S4D-100, but we are not able to edit the project S4D-300 &S4D-100.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Using the Migration Project in Different Client
entry.content:
The best approach of using the Migration Project in different client is by using the option "Use the project in Different Client" option.

Kindly follow the KBA 3501633 - Use Project in Different Client causes error "Project not found; Use F4 help to select a Project" for your reference.

Please try to change the Migration Project in client 100 via this option and then try to make the changes in the Project then only it will work.

entry.title: Table Grouping in Fiori Elements
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm developing an application using fiori elements, with a template list page and object page.

I would like to group the table in the object page by Storage Location. It would be a regular grouping, without aggregation. I've tried using CDS annotations and XML annotations, without success.

In the annotation target, I've tried with the entity itself and also its container.

<Annotations Target="SAP__self.Container/zz_pv_stock_zite_assets">
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="GroupBy">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>StorageLocation</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.LineItem</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>

            <Annotations Target="SAP__self.zz_pv_stock_zite_assetsType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="GroupBy">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>StorageLocation</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.LineItem</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>

It doesn't work, however the grouping works when I do directly in the app using the engine icon.

What could be missing?

Thank you in advance, Rogerio

entry.title: Re: Does SAP HANA graph have gremlin query support? If ye...
entry.content: Hej, there are no plans for Gremlin support in the SAP HANA property graph engine. So, for pattern matching queries there is Cypher, for graph procedures there is "GraphScript". However, with QRC1 of SAP HANA Cloud, we will provide support for knowledge graphs in terms of RDF triple store and SPARQL query engine.

entry.title: Mapping issues - Actions Builder - SAP Build Automation
entry.content:
Hello ,

I'm having a flow in SAP Build Automation where I need to extract information of a pdf file by using SAP Document Information Extraction service. In SAP Build Automation I'm using the SAP GET API call for this (SAP Business Accelerator Hub). Afterwards, I need to transfer the output to SAP CPI. I'm using a post API for this where I just need to push the output as a raw body input.

I made those 2 API's in SAP Build Action Builder and defined the output schema for the GET API by using the 'generate output' option in Action Builder. For the POST API call, I used the output of the GET API to add the input schema (Fields from sample JSON).

I wanted to test this it in Action Builder and I came to the conclusion that I'm receiving this problem?

When I check my input schema I see this: 

My question now: How is it possible if I just use the output of my GET API as input in the POST API, that I says that there are mapping problems? 

Kind regards,

Cédric

entry.title: Re: App "Create Advance Return on Sales/Purchase Tax" - F...
entry.content:
Since BAdI FINS_ADV_RETURNS_SALES is not available in the SAP Public Cloud,
you need to use Custom Fields and Logic (Extensibility) in SAP Fiori.

Correct Solution for SAP Public Cloud:
1. Use Key User Extensibility: Navigate to "Custom Fields and Logic" in SAP Fiori.
2. Enhance Business Context: Select Billing Document and create custom fields if needed.
3. Use Custom Logic (BAdI): Implement the relevant Cloud BAdI like FINS_ADV_RETURNS_SALES_CLOUD (if available) or check for tax-related extensibility options.
4. Define Mapping Logic: Use "Custom Logic" to pull values from VBRK/VBRP into user fields.
5. Test and Validate: Ensure data appears correctly in the Advance Return on Sales/Purchase Tax app.
For exact BAdI availability, check SAP Help Portal or raise an SAP Incident (OSS Note) for guidance on Cloud-compatible extensibility options.

entry.title: Adding Value Help (additional annotations) to Generated CDS views
entry.content:
Hi Dear SAP Community,

We are currently trying to create a report using the "Manage KPI and Reports" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

We followed these steps:

  1. Generate a custom CDS view using the "Custom CDS View" app.
  2. Create a query based on the CDS view in the "Custom Analytical Queries" app.
  3. Generate the report using the "Manage KPI and Reports" app.

Everything works fine until one of our customers requested value help for certain fields.

As seen, we don’t have any value help for the Project ID.

Below my demo Custom CDS view just to show what I tried.

Is there any chance of adding value help to this report, or are there possible extension options?

I tried to copy the generated CDS query and add value help there. Obviously, it didn’t work because we are not allowed to use unreleased objects.

 

Thank you very much in advance,

Best regards,

Rufat

entry.title: Re: Handling Images in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
"data:image/png;base64," + SUBSTRING(data.Employees1.Photo, 104)

i am getting data in base 64 and I am bind with this method and it working fine when we preview the APP but when we deploy app to the workzone image url is converting again in some encoded data can anyone please help me

entry.title: SAP_Build_Apps- Web and Mobile Application
entry.content: In SAP build apps how can create formula for when i select one value in dropdown-list the automatically field in other field..?

entry.title: Re: I receive the error message Bank account number not v...
entry.content:
Please check this note
2867786 - Dutch IBAN change as of 1st January 2020 - SAP for Me

" in the Netherlands, the account number has been completely replaced by the IBAN. This means that all account numbers are invalid and cannot be used in payments anymore. So the best approach is to activate the function 'IBAN without account number' and not to use any account numbers anymore, maintaining just the IBAN. "

entry.title: Re: SAP Utilities Roadmap 2025 Onwards
entry.content: Thank you @holger_schweinfurth  for the article!

entry.title: Mandatory field can be saved without content
entry.content:
While creating any external field via KUT and you use Field type as Decimal Number, after clicking on save it will not throw an error even the field is set as mandatory.

This is a known limitation in Byd.

If you want the mandatory check to be happen then based on your requirement you need to select the string field like any Text 120 field or Extended Text.

entry.title: Re: Using the Migration Project in Different Client
entry.content:
Apologies,

Please refer to the KBA 3081417 - How to use a migration project in a different client in SAP S/4HANA system? instead of the provided KBA before

entry.title: Re: How to get ClientIP in Sap commerce cloud Backend.
entry.content: Hi Cieslo, Thanks for your response. We are not using any cdn currently.Requests are directly getting handled through CCV2.So which parameter should i refer in that case
